prompt.task: Generate a batch of social media posts based on the source content.
prompt.context: This is part of a content repurposing pipeline. Long-form content (blog posts, podcasts, reports) is transformed into social posts. The output feeds directly into a scheduling tool via API, so strict format compliance is required. Brand: B2B SaaS company, professional but not stuffy.

constraints.constraint: All content must be derived from the source material.
Do not invent statistics or quotes not in the source.
Each platform's content must feel native to that platform.

prompt.input: Source: Blog post titled "Why We Switched from Jira to Linear" Key points: - Reduced sprint planning time by 40% - Team satisfaction scores improved from 6.2 to 8.1 - Migration took 2 weeks with full history preserved - Main benefits: speed, keyboard shortcuts, better defaults - Main tradeoff: less customization, smaller ecosystem URL: https://example.com/blog/jira-to-linear

output_contract.format: Return valid JSON matching the schema below. No markdown, no additional commentary.

constraints_on_output.constraint: LinkedIn posts must have a strong hook_line (question or bold statement).
Twitter threads must be 3-7 tweets; single tweets for simple announcements.
All char_count values must be accurate.
Hashtags: 3-5 for LinkedIn, 1-2 for Twitter, 0 for Threads.
Generate exactly 2 LinkedIn variants, 1 Twitter thread, and 1 Threads post.

output_contract.example_output:
{
  "source_url": "https://example.com/blog/jira-to-linear",
  "generated_at": "2025-01-17T10:30:00Z",
  "content_batch": {
    "linkedin": [
      {
        "post_type": "text",
        "content": "We cut our sprint planning time by 40%.

Not by hiring more people.
Not by adding another tool.

By removing one.

After 3 years on Jira, we switched to Linear. Here's what changed:

→ Sprint planning: 2 hours → 45 minutes
→ Team satisfaction: 6.2 → 8.1
→ Time to find anything: seconds, not minutes

The biggest surprise? The migration only took 2 weeks—with full history preserved.

The tradeoff: less customization. But honestly? We didn't need 90% of what we were customizing.

Sometimes the best optimization is subtraction.

Full breakdown in comments 👇",
        "hashtags": ["#ProductManagement", "#Engineering", "#Productivity", "#Startups"],
        "hook_line": "We cut our sprint planning time by 40%.",
        "cta": "Full breakdown in comments",
        "estimated_read_time": "45 sec"
      },
      {
        "post_type": "text",
        "content": "Hot take: Your project management tool is probably too powerful.

We used Jira for 3 years. Customized everything. Built workflows for workflows.

Then we switched to Linear and realized:

The best tool isn't the most powerful one.
It's the one your team actually enjoys using.

Our results after 6 months:
• 40% faster sprint planning
• Team satisfaction up 30%
• Zero "how do I do X in Jira" Slack messages

Yes, we lost some customization.
No, we don't miss it.

Link to full story in comments.",
        "hashtags": ["#ProjectManagement", "#SaaS", "#TeamProductivity", "#DevTools"],
        "hook_line": "Hot take: Your project management tool is probably too powerful.",
        "cta": "Link to full story in comments",
        "estimated_read_time": "40 sec"
      }
    ],
    "twitter": [
      {
        "post_type": "thread",
        "tweets": [
          {
            "content": "We switched from Jira to Linear after 3 years.

Sprint planning went from 2 hours to 45 minutes.

Here's what we learned (and what we lost):

🧵",
            "char_count": 168
          },
          {
            "content": "The main wins:

→ Everything is faster (keyboard-first design)
→ Better defaults = less configuration
→ Team satisfaction: 6.2 → 8.1

We stopped fighting the tool.",
            "char_count": 178
          },
          {
            "content": "The migration was easier than expected:

• 2 weeks total
• Full history preserved
• No lost context

We expected months. We were wrong.",
            "char_count": 148
          },
          {
            "content": "The tradeoffs:

• Less customization (we didn't need it)
• Smaller ecosystem (fewer integrations)
• No portfolio-level views (yet)

For our 20-person team: worth it.",
            "char_count": 179
          },
          {
            "content": "Full breakdown here:

https://example.com/blog/jira-to-linear

If you're drowning in Jira configurations, it might be worth a look.",
            "char_count": 138
          }
        ],
        "thread_hook": "We switched from Jira to Linear after 3 years. Sprint planning went from 2 hours to 45 minutes."
      }
    ],
    "threads": [
      {
        "content": "Switched from Jira to Linear after 3 years. Sprint planning dropped from 2 hours to 45 minutes. Team satisfaction went from 6.2 to 8.1.

The tradeoff? Less customization. But we realized we were over-engineering our own workflows.

Sometimes simpler tools win.",
        "char_count": 289
      }
    ]
  },
  "posting_recommendations": {
    "best_time_suggestion": "LinkedIn: Tuesday-Thursday 8-10am; Twitter: Weekdays 12-1pm",
    "spacing_advice": "Post LinkedIn variants 3-4 days apart to avoid audience overlap fatigue",
    "a_b_test_ideas": [
      "Test 'hot take' hook vs. 'results' hook on LinkedIn",
      "Compare engagement on thread vs. single tweet with link"
    ]
  }
}
